note: Two regimes on the 8 GB M2. The fast path (-mmp 0 -ngl 99: weights in RAM, full Metal offload) is ~500x faster at decode but loading 4.4 GB resident on 8 GB swap-killed/froze the machine. The safe path (-mmp 1 -ngl 0: mmap, CPU) keeps RAM low and is stable but glacial — decode pages all 4.4 GB from the USB SSD per token.

gpu_ram.stability_note: completed the benchmark but loading 4.4 GB into 8 GB unified memory froze the system shortly after; not a safe operating point

cpu_mmap.stability_note: memory held at ~30% free throughout; no crash

decode_tps.note: 6 decode tokens did not finish in 200 s → < 0.03 tok/s; >30 s/token, dominated by USB-SSD page-ins of the 4.4 GB weights

headline: Decode throughput collapses 17.6 -> <0.03 tok/s (>500x) when the 8 GB wall forces weights off the GPU and onto demand-paging from the SSD. This is the memory-bound / paging result the lecture predicts.
